RFC 7908                     Route Leaks                    June 2016

2.  Route Leak Definition

   A route leak is the propagation of routing announcement(s) beyond
   their intended scope.  The result of a route leak can be redirection
   of traffic through an unintended path.

2.1.  Operational Consequences

   Operators SHOULD preserve the intended customer-provider boundary.
   Detection evidence needs the announced prefix, origin ASN, AS path,
   observation time, and collector identity.
